stories.headline: OpenAI delays GPT-5.6 release after White House security request
stories.blurb: The Trump administration asked OpenAI to slow down its next model launch over security concerns. CEO Sam Altman told employees the company will release GPT-5.6 in limited preview first, giving access to a small group before a wider rollout.
stories.why_it_matters: First time the US government has publicly intervened in an AI model release timeline.
stories.original_headline: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request

stories.headline: Claude is stealing ChatGPT's paying customers
stories.blurb: People who pay for AI subscriptions are switching to Anthropic's Claude, even though ChatGPT still dominates the overall market. The shift shows paying users—who actually use AI daily—are picking Claude over OpenAI's offering.
stories.why_it_matters: The users willing to pay are the ones who matter most, and they're voting with their wallets.
stories.original_headline: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT

stories.headline: Apple adds Google Gemini as a third coding assistant in Xcode
stories.blurb: Xcode 26.6 now supports Google Gemini alongside existing AI coding assistants, giving developers another option for AI-powered code completion and suggestions directly in Apple's IDE. Developers can now choose between multiple AI models while building iOS and Mac apps.
stories.why_it_matters: Apple is opening up its development tools to competing AI models instead of locking developers into one assistant.
stories.original_headline: Apple adds Google Gemini coding assistant in Xcode 26.6 update

stories.headline: Patronus AI raises $50M to build simulated worlds where AI agents fail
stories.blurb: Patronus AI, founded by ex-Meta researchers, creates digital environments designed to break AI agents before they break in production. The startup is building test scenarios that expose how agents behave under edge cases, adversarial inputs, and real-world chaos—essentially crash-test dummies for autonomous software.
stories.why_it_matters: As companies ship agents that book flights and manage money, knowing where they fail matters more than knowing where they work.
stories.original_headline: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents

stories.headline: AWS CEO says companies are moving AI past the pilot stage
stories.blurb: Matt Garman claims enterprises are shifting from AI experiments to production deployments that deliver measurable returns. The interview focuses on what's changed to make AI projects stick beyond proof-of-concept, though specific ROI examples aren't detailed in the available excerpt.
stories.why_it_matters: If true, it signals AI is crossing the gap from R&D budget to core business tool.
stories.original_headline: AWS CEO Matt Garman on why enterprise AI is finally delivering real returns

stories.headline: Developer let 2,000 people try to jailbreak his AI assistant — here's what broke
stories.blurb: A developer built an AI assistant with access to his calendar, Gmail, and Slack, then invited the internet to hack it. The results: prompt injection worked repeatedly, users extracted system prompts easily, and guardrails failed against creative attacks. He's now sharing the attack patterns and what actually stopped them.
stories.why_it_matters: Real-world jailbreak data from a production AI tool with actual access to sensitive systems.
stories.original_headline: What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant

try_this_prompt.title: The negotiation floor-and-ceiling
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm about to negotiate something I'll describe below. Don't give me tactics or scripts. Instead: tell me the worst deal I can accept without feeling like I lost, then tell me the best deal I can realistically ask for without seeming delusional. Then tell me which number to say out loud first.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you walk into salary talks, vendor pricing, or equity discussions.

daily_question: If you could stress-test one AI tool you use daily against 2,000 adversarial users, what would break first?

args.reason: THE defining AI story today - OpenAI, a major AI lab, at the center of a significant government action. Everyone in AI will be discussing this. When the Trump administration asks OpenAI to delay their next model release, that's mandatory slot 1 material - it's about the AI company and product, not AI as a footnote.
